channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: America's kids are still behind in reading and math. These schools are defying the trend
item.description: COMPTON, Calif. (AP) — Math is the subject sixth grader Harmoni Knight finds hardest, but that's changing. In-class tutors and “data chats” at her middle school in Compton, California, have made a dramatic difference, the 11-year-old said.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Roses are red, violets are blue, 940 million flowers are traveling (through Miami) to you
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers. In the run up to Feb. 14, agricultural specialists at Miami International Airport have processed about 940 million stems of cut flowers, according to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Migrants stranded in Mexico try to restart life after Trump eliminates legal pathway to US
item.description: Many migrants have been left stranded in Mexican border cities after the Trump administration immediately canceled tens of thosands of appointments made through a government app called CBP One that offered a legal pathway to the U.S. Some have returned to their countries.
item.dc:creator: Julie Watson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What the Westminster show dogs' names mean
item.description: They have names that could make a random password generator cry uncle. Meet, for example, GCHG CH Calicops Sassafras Gonnakikurass. “She’s a saucy girl. Her name says it all,” Fred Ortiz said as he groomed the Brussels griffon to compete Monday at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hegseth says Fort Bragg is coming back, but with a twist
item.description: On Monday, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth signed a memo that orders the Army to rename Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, this time in honor of a different soldier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Person killed in Union Station shooting during rush hour
item.description: WASHINGTON (DC News Now) -- A person was shot and killed during rush hour Monday afternoon at D.C.'s Union Station. The United States Capitol Police (USCP) said officers responded to Union Station at around 4:40 p.m., where they found one person who had been shot.
item.dc:creator: Gabby Allen, NewsNation

item.title: Trump fires Office of Government Ethics chief
item.description: President Trump has fired the director of the Office of Government Ethics, the agency announced Monday. The office posted on its website that it had been notified Trump was removing David Huitema, who had been nominated by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hoarder arrested for extreme animal cruelty, as authorities discover 10 dead pets, rescue many more: police
item.description: A Long Island woman was arrested and charged with animal cruelty and other charges after a search warrant on her residence revealed unlivable conditions for dozens of animals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illegal alien charged in sandwich shop owner's murder caught and released into US under Biden: ICE
item.description: An illegal alien from the Dominican Republic has been charged with the home invasion murder of a Massachusetts sandwich shop owner named Ilias "Louie" Mavros.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he has directed US Treasury to stop minting new pennies, citing rising cost
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he has directed the Treasury Department to stop minting new pennies, citing the rising cost of producing the one-cent coin. “For far too long the United States has minted pennies which literally cost us more than 2 cents.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mother orders son to shoot her boyfriend after argument: Police
item.description: The victim said that Prentis Lee approached his vehicle armed with a black handgun allegedly saying, "You got my momma f----- up. I will shoot you in your face.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, NewsNation

item.title: So long, penny! Trump orders US to ditch 1-cent coin after decades of complaints
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — The lowly penny, the forgotten mainstay of coin jars and car cupholders everywhere, may soon be no more. President Donald Trump announced Sunday he’s ordered his administration to cease production of the 1-cent coin, whose buying power is long past its prime.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brother of Parkland shooter arrested for trespassing at three schools
item.description: Surveillance footage recovered from Jan. 25 showed him attempting to open exterior doors and peering into the schools.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Sutherland, NewsNation

item.title: Judge finds Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending
item.description: A federal judge found Monday that the Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending and told the White House to release all the money.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trader Joe's is now limiting egg sales nationwide
item.description: Trader Joe’s patrons nationwide have taken to social media to comment on a new policy that limits the amount of eggs shoppers can buy. The company told Nexstar's KOIN that limits are currently in place at all of its U.S. locations.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, KTLA 5

item.title: GOP senators 'terrified' of crossing Trump, facing Musk-funded challengers
item.description: GOP senators are "terrified" over the prospect of facing primary challengers funded by Elon Musk if they stick their necks out by opposing President Donald Trump's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Eagles running back Saquon Barkley sets the NFL's season rushing record, including the playoffs
item.description: Saquon Barkley set an NFL single-season rushing record in the Super Bowl, held his young daughter as a torrent of confetti fluttered around them and flashed a smile as he held a Philadelphia newspaper with “CHAMPS!” stamped above his photo.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A presidential first: Trump at the Super Bowl, latest chapter in a complicated legacy with football
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — As a student, Donald Trump played high school football. As a business baron, he owned a team in an upstart rival to the NFL and then sued the established league. As president, he denigrated pros who took a knee during the national anthem as part of a social justice movement.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says 25 percent tariffs on steel and aluminum coming Monday
item.description: Trump said that the steel and aluminum tariffs would impact “everybody” when asked what countries would be affected.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Snow blankets parts of Northeast US, up to a foot expected for some
item.description: A strong storm is expected to bring moderate to heavy snowfall across parts of the Upper Great Lakes on Saturday before intensifying overnight across the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Marine from California identified as one of 4 people killed in Philippines plane crash
item.description: CAMP PENDLETON, Calif. (AP) — A U.S. Marine from California was identified by military officials Sunday as one of four people killed when a plane contracted by the U.S. military crashed in a rice field in the southern Philippines. Sgt. Jacob M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Camp Pendleton identifies Marine killed in Philippines surveillance mission
item.description: Marine Corps officials at Camp Pendleton on Sunday identified Sgt. Jacob M. Durham as the U.S. Marine who tragically lost his life in an aircraft incident on Feb. 6, 2025, in the Philippine Province of Maguindanao del Sur.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said. The Nome Volunteer Fire Department made the announcement on its Facebook page Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Stage and film actor Tony Roberts dies at 85
item.description: Tony Roberts, a versatile, Tony Award-nominated theater performer at home in both plays and musicals and who appeared in several Woody Allen movies — often as Allen's best friend — has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are working to recover the wreckage of a plane crash in western Alaska that killed 10 people, while investigators are trying to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ohio city files federal lawsuit against alleged neo-Nazi group
item.description: The city of Springfield, Ohio has filed a lawsuit against an alleged neo-Nazi group for conducting what it claims to be a campaign of threats and intimidation.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, ABC 4

item.title: Longtime NFL player and coach Dick Jauron dies at 74
item.description: Longtime NFL player and coach Dick Jauron, who led the Chicago Bears to the playoffs and was voted AP coach of the year in 2001, died Saturday. He was 74.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Family files lawsuit after mother, son die from food poisoning while on vacation
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, WGN 9

item.title: Super Bowl prices are down this year. StubHub explains why
item.description: If you’re still planning to attend the Super Bowl on Sunday, you can expect a price drop compared to last year.
item.dc:creator: JOHN CARUCCI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jim Becker, AP reporter who covered Jackie Robinson and an underdog Hawaii football team, dies at 98
item.description: Jim Becker, a world-traveling journalist who covered Jackie Robinson’s big-league baseball debut and the U.S. Army’s retaking of Seoul during the Korean War, died Friday. He was 98.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: West Virginia leads 22 states suing over New York Climate Change Superfund Act
item.description: West Virginia led the charge against New York’s Climate Change Superfund Act, suing to block the law.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, Fox 5

item.title: States absorb big increases in Medicaid for sicker-than-expected enrollees after COVID-19 pandemic
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — States are absorbing substantial increases in health care costs for the poor, as they realize that the people remaining on Medicaid rolls after the COVID-19 pandemic are sicker than anticipated — and costlier to care for.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Mexico courtroom devolves into brawl as 3 people rush defendant in homicide case
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A hearing in a New Mexico courtroom devolved into chaos when three people rushed the defendant in a homicide case, setting off a brawl with flying fists and kicks. Courtroom video cameras recorded the Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
